title: potentilla argentea subsp. calabra
intro: Potentilla argentea subsp. calabra, also known as Calabrian cinquefoil, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae, characterized by its silver-hued leaves and clusters of small, yellow flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making it useful in treating various health issues such as wounds, fever, and gastrointestinal problems. The health benefits of Potentilla argentea subsp. calabra include reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and supporting the immune system, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions and promote overall well-being.
